name: Flaming Mountains
description: The Flaming Mountains, also known as the Huoyan Mountains, are a barren, eroded, red sandstone mountain range located near Turpan in the Xinjiang region of China. These mountains are famously depicted in the classic Chinese novel *Journey to the West*. In the novel, the mountains are the result of Sun Wukong (the Monkey King) kicking over a furnace while rebelling against Heaven. The flames that spilled out became the mountains, creating an impassable barrier on the journey to retrieve Buddhist scriptures. The intense heat emanating from the mountains, reaching scorching temperatures during the summer, makes them a formidable obstacle. The mythology surrounding the Flaming Mountains emphasizes the power of divine wrath and the challenges faced by pilgrims seeking enlightenment. They represent a tangible manifestation of past transgressions and a test of perseverance. To pass through them, the Tang Sanzang and his disciples needed to extinguish the flames, a task requiring the intervention of powerful deities and magical artifacts. The mountains serve as a reminder of the consequences of actions and the importance of overcoming obstacles on the path to spiritual growth. The landscape itself reinforces this theme, with its stark beauty and unforgiving climate presenting a constant challenge to those who dare to traverse it. The legend has made the real-life Flaming Mountains a popular tourist destination, drawing visitors who seek to experience the mythical landscape firsthand.
subtitle: Fiery barrier on the Silk Road

keyMyths.title: Sun Wukong's Furnace
keyMyths.description: The Flaming Mountains were created when Sun Wukong, during his rebellion in Heaven, kicked over a furnace, scattering embers that formed the fiery mountain range. This act of defiance resulted in a lasting obstacle for future travelers.

keyMyths.title: The Iron Fan Princess
keyMyths.description: To extinguish the flames of the Flaming Mountains, Tang Sanzang and his disciples needed the Iron Fan, possessed by Princess Iron Fan, the wife of the Bull Demon King. Obtaining the fan required cunning and perseverance from Sun Wukong.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: A classic Chinese novel that prominently features the Flaming Mountains.
sources.text: A classic Chinese novel that prominently features the Flaming Mountains.
